name: The Guiding Star Imperial Academy
description:
Guiding Star, a place in where talent and nobility collide, with magic knowledge in the middle, and greatness as the aim.

A holy land for anyone wanting to learn magic and discover all its secrets.

As long as you have the talent, or the coin, you're welcome to join the halls, be it for ambition, duty or curiosity.

Welcome to the academy.

The place

The Imperial Academy is as massive as it is impressive. A massive array of building all made of pearly white bricks, connected by massive halls and beautiful gardens.

Maybe you want to spend your day practicing in The Training Grounds, honing your magic, or maybe even your form with your weapon of choice?

Or maybe you want to study the secrets of magic and mana in the Embers Library, the greatest and most complete collection on magical knowledge in all of the known world?

You can spend your evening's in the Mess Hall, a place that serves food fit for royalty on a daily basis, with great service provided by the staff.

And you can take a walk between classes on the stunning gardens maintained by the botany department, filled with exotic flowers and impressive herbs.

Or if you wanna get a more hands on experience, there are workshops for artificing and alchemy on the Steel Wing that use the best of the best equipment and setups, making even difficult and complex tasks feel simple.

And for those who are in awe at the beauty of magical beasts, The Pens hold some of the most impressive and rare ones in the continent, all ready to be observed, studied and even interacted with.

Essentials to know about the academy

Magisters: The peacekeepers of the academy. The monitor the halls and ensure the safety of the students, both from internal and external threats.

Class Modality: Classes in the guiding star are all optional. The only students that are required to take periodical examinations are Scholarship students. A students is free to take any classes they want. Graduation examinations are demonstrations of what students have learned in their years of the academy, and the teachers decide on if they're worthy or not of the honor of doing so. For non scholarship students this is usually only done for prestige reasons.

Scholarships: Due to the high cost of tuition, which is 50 gold coins per year, most commoners are unable to afford to pay for it. Scholarships exist as a way to aid commoners in exchange for future benefits. The academy will offer scholarships to any promising students that are willing to work as staff in the academy after graduation.
